threats.title: Shared or weak database credentials
threats.description: A single, weak, or widely-known credential fronts the database, so any component (or a leaked secret) grants full access.

threats.title: SQL injection reaches the query layer
threats.description: Application input concatenated into SQL lets an attacker read or alter arbitrary rows.

threats.title: Data changes are not audited
threats.description: Row-level changes leave no trail, so a malicious or erroneous mutation can't be traced.

threats.title: Data at rest is unencrypted or backups are exposed
threats.description: Sensitive columns and backup snapshots sit in cleartext, so a disk or backup compromise reads them directly.

threats.title: Over-privileged database account
threats.description: The application connects as a superuser, so an injection or app compromise inherits far more than least privilege.
